task_completion.description: Verify the agent installed or imported a QR code generation library.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing 'pip install qrcode' or a Python script containing 'import qrcode'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent accessed the Aerobridge database to retrieve aircraft data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a sqlite3 query, a Django shell session, or a Python script querying the 'registry_aircraft' and 'registry_aircraftdetail' tables to get UUIDs and registration marks.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent constructed the correct Django admin URLs for the QR codes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Python script or command showing the URL pattern 'http://localhost:8000/admin/registry/aircraft/<uuid>/change/' being formatted with the retrieved UUIDs.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent saved the outputs to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A script containing the path '/home/ga/Documents/fleet_tags/' or terminal commands navigating to/listing this directory.

task_completion.description: Verify exactly two PNG files were created with the correct registration mark filenames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing 'ls' output or a file manager in '/home/ga/Documents/fleet_tags/' displaying exactly two files: 'tag_FE-2212.png' and 'tag_FE-21.png'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually executed a script or command to generate the images.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution of the Python script (e.g., 'python generate_qrs.py') or interactive shell commands that perform the generation, rather than files just appearing.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent extracted the UUIDs and registration marks from the database rather than hallucinating or hardcoding them without querying.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python script should contain database connection/ORM logic (e.g., sqlite3.connect or Django ORM imports) rather than just having the UUIDs '0797d9ef...' and '0450852f...' hardcoded from the start.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent generated actual image files, not empty text files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The script should show actual QR code generation logic (e.g., 'img.save()'), or an 'ls -l' command should show the .png files have a file size greater than 0 bytes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The database contains exactly 2 aircraft instances. Their registration marks are 'FE-2212' and 'FE-21', and their UUIDs are '0797d9ef-02d9-41cf-93ff-439aaf1609ae' and '0450852f-856e-4ecb-beb6-01ccded8529d'. The expected output files are 'tag_FE-2212.png' and 'tag_FE-21.png' located in '/home/ga/Documents/fleet_tags/'. The QR codes must encode URLs matching 'http://localhost:8000/admin/registry/aircraft/<uuid>/change/'.
